stories.headline: Iran War Will Make EU More Reliant on US Gas Than Ever: IEEFA
stories.summary: Europe’s reliance on natural gas from the US is expected to surge to a record this year as the country helps offset supplies lost from the Middle East, according to an energy think tank.

stories.summary: Moscow and Kyiv lose faith in Donald Trump’s diplomacy even in the event of Iran war ending

stories.headline: Pentagon says US has spent $29 billion on Iran war so far, amid concerns over weapons stockpiles
stories.summary: Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth came under intense questioning by Democratic and Republican lawmakers over the cost of war, Trump's end game and diminishing weapons stockpiles.

stories.headline: The Iran war and the future of cheap flights
stories.summary: Low-cost air travel may be a thing of the past as rising fuel prices tip carriers into another crisis

stories.headline: FBI Director Kash Patel fires back at drinking allegations
stories.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel and Senator Chris Van Hollen had a heated exchange during a Senate budget hearing.

stories.headline: Will Singapore warm to nuclear as 20% of electricity goes to data centers?
Trump-Xi summit: Chips may be off the table but AI warfare will likely feature in China
stories.summary: As Trump heads to Beijing for a summit with Xi Jinping, AI-enabled warfare, cybersecurity and the deepening US-China tech rivalry are set to dominate talks, even as a breakthrough deal on semiconductors looks unlikely.

stories.headline: India More Than Doubles Gold, Silver Tariffs to Defend Rupee
stories.summary: India has raised import tariffs on gold and silver in an attempt to defend its currency, as the country races to shore up foreign-exchange reserves and limit the damage from the war in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Pettis on China's Growth Woes, Global Trade Imbalances
stories.summary: Carnegie Endowment for International Peace Senior Fellow Michael Pettis discusses his outlook for the summit between Chinese President Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump. He also offers insights on the challenges of rebalancing trade. He speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Korean Stocks Rebound as Retail Traders Counter Foreign Selling
stories.summary: South Korean stocks rapidly erased losses to climb toward fresh highs Wednesday, as local retail investors bought into a wave of selling by foreign funds.

stories.headline: Australia Fin Min on Federal Budget
stories.summary: Katy Gallagher, Australian Minister for Finance, says "uncertainty has made it more challenging to land the federal budget" as the impact of the Iran war comes through, prompting the government to focus on issues like securing fuel security, and resilience. She speaks with Bloomberg's Paul Allen in Canberra on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade". (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Nvidia C.E.O. Jensen Huang Hitches Ride With Trump to China After Last-Minute Invite
stories.summary: Jensen Huang boarded Air Force One in Alaska, joining a delegation of more than a dozen business leaders accompanying President Trump on his trip to Beijing.

stories.headline: China’s Profit Outlook to Improve Further, Morgan Stanley Says
stories.summary: Chinese companies’ second‑quarter profit outlook is set to improve after encouraging first‑quarter results, with rising exports and early signs of reflation boosting revenues, according to Morgan Stanley.

stories.headline: Memory Crunch Deepens Chasm Between Stock Winners and Losers
stories.summary: The worsening shortage in global memory chips due to the artificial intelligence buildout is driving a widening gulf in corporate results and stock performances.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley Sees Yuan Rally Slowing, Diverging From Bulls
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley sees room for the yuan to climb further, but disagrees with more bullish views that the currency is significantly undervalued.

stories.headline: China Sees Its Soy Imports Sliding Just as US Farmers Eye Deals
stories.summary: China, the world’s top soybean importer, expects purchases to slump in the coming season as its pig herd declines, heightening competition for market share just as the US seeks to ramp up sales.

stories.headline: Australian Productivity Chair on Federal Budget
stories.summary:
Danielle Wood, Australian Productivity Commission Chair, discusses her thoughts on the national budget and its implications for boosting productivity in Australia. She speaks with Bloomberg's Paul Allen in Canberra on "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade".
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: A recovery in tech shares may be slow, Bajaj Broking says.

stories.headline: Trump Says Americans’ Economic Pain Is Not a Consideration for the Iran War
stories.summary: Asked whether Americans’ financial situation was motivating him to make a deal, President Trump said bluntly, “Not even a little bit.”

stories.headline: Pakistan struck a rehab centre and killed 269 Afghans. Their families want to know why

stories.headline: Kash Patel Denies Lying and Excessive Drinking in Heated Exchanges With Democrats

stories.headline: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy not implicated in a major graft probe, anti-corruption investigators say
stories.summary: “The President of Ukraine has neither been nor is currently a subject of the investigation,” the head of Ukraine’s anti-corruption bureau said on Tuesday, as the president’s former chief of staff appeared in court for a bail hearing.

stories.headline: Why is Iran increasingly targeting the UAE in its war messaging?

stories.headline: Food prices could skyrocket, if no measures taken to tackle fertilizer crisis, farmer unions warn
stories.summary: The fertilizer crisis caused by the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is tripling costs for farmers. “Less fertilizers means less production for wheat. That’s the basis for bread,” warns COPA President, Massimiliano Giansanti.

stories.headline: Air India crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report

stories.headline: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes

stories.headline: EU needs to delay social media access for children - von der Leyen
stories.summary: She told an EU summit that an expert panel was due to come up with steps on how to protect minors online by July.

stories.headline: Ex-Philippine leader Duterte's drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest
stories.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, a former national police chief, has sought refuge in the senate.

stories.headline: MSCI Removes Stocks Linked to Indonesia’s Richest From Indexes
stories.summary: MSCI Inc. cut some Indonesian stocks linked to the country’s richest billionaires from its indexes, following through on a warning last month that it will exclude companies with concentrated ownership. The shares fell.

stories.headline: US Soy Growers Want China Commitments as Season Slips Away
stories.summary: As US President Donald Trump travels to Beijing for a summit this week with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, American soybean farmers are in a now-familiar spot: Fields are getting planted when demand from their biggest customer is uncertain.

stories.headline: Kevin Warsh confirmed to US Federal Reserve board in close Senate vote
stories.summary: Senator John Fetterman of Pennsylvania, a Democrat, joins Republicans to advance Warsh's confirmation.

stories.headline: Chinese people divided ahead of Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.headline: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
stories.summary: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.

stories.headline: Uganda's president sworn in for record seventh term
stories.summary: Yoweri Museveni, 81, won disputed elections in January to extend his 40-year rule.

stories.headline: South Africa's top court bars repeat asylum applications
stories.summary: The home affairs minister hails the ruling as a "victory" against the "abuse" of the refugee system.

stories.headline: Indigenous Australians win record A$150m after billionaire mined without permission

stories.headline: Trump ally Kari Lake tapped to be US ambassador to Jamaica
stories.summary: Lake thanked President Trump for nominating her, saying on social media that Jamaica "is a country I know very well, full of incredible people".

stories.headline: Jason Collins, First Active N.B.A. Player to Come Out as Gay, Dies at 47
stories.summary: His achievements on the court were eclipsed by an essay he wrote in Sports Illustrated in 2013 in which he declared: “I’m a 34-year-old N.B.A. center. I’m Black and I’m gay.”

stories.headline: SOCAR acquisition of Italy’s Italiana Petroli finalised after EU clearance
stories.summary: SOCAR has completed its acquisition of Italiana Petroli after securing all regulatory approvals, marking a major expansion of its downstream presence in Italy and strengthening its position in Europe’s energy market.

stories.headline: EU to ban Brazilian meat imports from September
stories.summary: The ban would come just two weeks after the EU-Mercosur trade deal provisionally entered into force, liberalising agricultural trade across the Atlantic despite fierce opposition from farmers.

stories.headline: Trump calls Iran response to US proposal to end war 'totally unacceptable'
stories.summary: Iran is reported to want lifting of the US naval blockade, recognition of Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and compensation for war damage.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on ‘life support,’ calls latest proposal ‘garbage’

stories.headline: Philippine VP Sara Duterte impeached for a second time
stories.summary: If convicted in a Senate trial, which is uncertain, she will be disqualified from running for president.

stories.headline: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence

stories.headline: Advocaat, 78, set for World Cup record after Curacao return
stories.summary: Dick Advocaat makes a surprise return as Curacao's head coach after his successor Fred Rutten resigned just a month out from their World Cup debut.

stories.headline: Confident in China’s power, Xi is ready to host an unpredictable Trump
stories.summary: Holding no illusions about making lasting deals at this week’s summit, China’s leader looks to project Beijing as an alternative to U.S. volatility on the world stage.

stories.headline: Why welfare isn't winning elections in India like it used to
stories.summary: India's welfare politics still matters, but voters increasingly want jobs, growth and dignity too.

stories.headline: GOP redistricting splintering Florida community angers Republicans and Democrats
stories.summary: Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said population growth necessitated redistricting Florida’s House map. But an overhaul of a majority-Hispanic area sparked a backlash.

stories.summary: Though Vijay campaigned only briefly, his fan club-turned-digital “warriors” amplified him online tirelessly.
